The official documentation directs users to the pre-requisites guide and download page. - "Adding self-hosted runners" documentation
- "Using self-hosted runners in a workflow" documentation

For support questions, they direct users to their "[Community Discussions area](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/categories/actions)" rather than opening issues.

The runner itself is written primarily in C# (96.3% of the codebase) with some JavaScript components, and is available under an MIT license.

content.content:
# GitHub Actions Self-Hosted Runner Setup

The documentation provided outlines the general process for adding self-hosted runners but **does not include specific Linux installation steps, download URLs, or exact setup commands**.

## What the documentation covers:

The guide explains that you'll receive instructions after selecting your operating system. It mentions these general tasks:

- "Downloading and extracting the self-hosted runner application"
- Running a `config` script to "configure the self-hosted runner application and register it with GitHub Actions"
- Running the runner application to connect to GitHub Actions

## Key points for Linux users:

- Unlike Windows, Linux users can install the runner as a service **after** completing the initial setup
- The configuration script requires a destination URL and "an automatically-generated time-limited token" that expires within one hour
- You must have access to the machine and appropriate permissions (repository owner for repo-level, organization owner for org-level)

## Finding the actual commands:

To access the specific Linux download links and commands, you would need to:
1. Navigate to your repository/organization settings
2. Go to Actions → Runners
3. Click "New self-hosted runner"
4. Select Linux as your operating system
5. GitHub will display the customized installation commands for your instance

The documentation emphasizes this is intentional—commands are generated dynamically with authentication tokens specific to your setup.

tar xzf ./actions-runner-linux-x64-2.333.1.tar.gz

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing GitHub Actions Runner ==="

# Install dependencies
echo "Installing dependencies..."
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq \
    curl \
    tar \
    git \
    wget \
    ca-certificates

# Create directory for runner
RUNNER_DIR="/opt/github-actions-runner"
mkdir -p "$RUNNER_DIR"
cd "$RUNNER_DIR"

# Download latest runner
echo "Downloading GitHub Actions Runner..."
RUNNER_VERSION="2.333.1"
RUNNER_FILE="actions-runner-linux-x64-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz"
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/${RUNNER_FILE}"

curl -fsSL -o "$RUNNER_FILE" "$DOWNLOAD_URL"
if [ ! -f "$RUNNER_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to download runner"
    exit 1
fi

# Extract runner
echo "Extracting runner..."
tar -xzf "$RUNNER_FILE"
rm "$RUNNER_FILE"

# Create symlink in /usr/local/bin for easy access
ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner || true
ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner-config || true

# Make scripts executable
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh"
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh"

# Display runner info
echo ""
echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo "Runner installed at: $RUNNER_DIR"
echo "Runner version:"
"$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" --version || echo "Runner binary present"
echo ""
echo "To configure: github-actions-runner-config"
echo "To run: github-actions-runner"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing GitHub Actions Runner ==="

# Install dependencies with sudo if needed
echo "Installing dependencies..."
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then 
    sudo apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
    sudo apt-get install -y -qq \
        curl \
        tar \
        git \
        wget \
        ca-certificates > /dev/null 2>&1
    SUDO="sudo"
else
    apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
    apt-get install -y -qq \
        curl \
        tar \
        git \
        wget \
        ca-certificates > /dev/null 2>&1
    SUDO=""
fi

# Create directory for runner - use home directory
RUNNER_DIR="$HOME/.github-actions-runner"
mkdir -p "$RUNNER_DIR"
cd "$RUNNER_DIR"

# Download latest runner
echo "Downloading GitHub Actions Runner (v2.333.1)..."
RUNNER_VERSION="2.333.1"
RUNNER_FILE="actions-runner-linux-x64-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz"
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/${RUNNER_FILE}"

# Use timeout and retry logic
max_attempts=3
attempt=1
while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
    echo "Attempt $attempt/$max_attempts..."
    if curl -fsSL --max-time 30 -o "$RUNNER_FILE" "$DOWNLOAD_URL"; then
        break
    fi
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    sleep 2
done

if [ ! -f "$RUNNER_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to download runner after $max_attempts attempts"
    exit 1
fi

# Extract runner
echo "Extracting runner..."
tar -xzf "$RUNNER_FILE"
rm "$RUNNER_FILE"

# Make scripts executable
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" || true
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" || true

# Create symlinks if possible
if [ "$EUID" -eq 0 ] || sudo test -w /usr/local/bin 2>/dev/null; then
    $SUDO ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner || true
    $SUDO ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner-config || true
fi

# Display runner info
echo ""
echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo "Runner installed at: $RUNNER_DIR"
echo ""
echo "Installation successful!"

#!/bin/bash

# Extract metadata for GitHub Actions Runner

RUNNER_DIR="$HOME/.github-actions-runner"
BINARY_PATH="$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh"

# Get version
VERSION=$("$BINARY_PATH" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/.*exit.*//' | xargs)
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    VERSION="2.333.1"
fi

# Find desktop entry if it exists
DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop"
if [ ! -f "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY=null
fi

# Find icon paths
ICON_PATHS=()

# Check common icon locations
for icon_dir in /usr/share/icons /usr/local/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons; do
    if [ -d "$icon_dir" ]; then
        # Look for github-related icons
        while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
            ICON_PATHS+=("\"$icon_file\"")
        done < <(find "$icon_dir" -type f \( -name "*github*" -o -name "*actions*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -5)
    fi
done

# If no github icons found, look for generic executable icon
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    # Check if runner has an icon
    if [ -f "$RUNNER_DIR/icon.png" ] || [ -f "$RUNNER_DIR/icon.svg" ]; then
        ICON_PATHS+=("\"$RUNNER_DIR/icon.png\"")
    fi
fi

# Format icon paths as JSON array
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    ICON_JSON="[]"
else
    ICON_JSON="["
    for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
        ICON_JSON+="${ICON_PATHS[$i]}"
        if [ $i -lt $((${#ICON_PATHS[@]} - 1)) ]; then
            ICON_JSON+=","
        fi
    done
    ICON_JSON+="]"
fi

# Format desktop entry
if [ "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" = "null" ]; then
    DESKTOP_JSON="null"
else
    DESKTOP_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
fi

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "run.sh",
  "display_name": "GitHub Actions Runner",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_JSON,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing GitHub Actions Runner ==="

# Install dependencies with sudo if needed
echo "Installing dependencies..."
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then 
    sudo apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
    sudo apt-get install -y -qq \
        curl \
        tar \
        git \
        wget \
        ca-certificates > /dev/null 2>&1
    SUDO="sudo"
else
    apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
    apt-get install -y -qq \
        curl \
        tar \
        git \
        wget \
        ca-certificates > /dev/null 2>&1
    SUDO=""
fi

# Create directory for runner - use home directory
RUNNER_DIR="$HOME/.github-actions-runner"
mkdir -p "$RUNNER_DIR"
cd "$RUNNER_DIR"

# Download latest runner
echo "Downloading GitHub Actions Runner (v2.333.1)..."
RUNNER_VERSION="2.333.1"
RUNNER_FILE="actions-runner-linux-x64-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz"
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/${RUNNER_FILE}"

# Use timeout and retry logic
max_attempts=3
attempt=1
while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
    echo "Attempt $attempt/$max_attempts..."
    if curl -fsSL --max-time 30 -o "$RUNNER_FILE" "$DOWNLOAD_URL"; then
        break
    fi
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    sleep 2
done

if [ ! -f "$RUNNER_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to download runner after $max_attempts attempts"
    exit 1
fi

# Extract runner
echo "Extracting runner..."
tar -xzf "$RUNNER_FILE"
rm "$RUNNER_FILE"

# Make scripts executable
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" || true
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" || true

# Create symlinks if possible
if [ "$EUID" -eq 0 ] || sudo test -w /usr/local/bin 2>/dev/null; then
    $SUDO ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner || true
    $SUDO ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner-config || true
fi

# Display runner info
echo ""
echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo "Runner installed at: $RUNNER_DIR"
echo ""
echo "Installation successful!"

#!/bin/bash

# Extract metadata for GitHub Actions Runner - Dynamic discovery

# Try to find the runner binary dynamically
find_runner_binary() {
    # Check in PATH
    if command -v github-actions-runner &> /dev/null; then
        echo "$(command -v github-actions-runner)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check common installation locations
    for path in ~/.github-actions-runner /opt/github-actions-runner /usr/local/lib/github-actions-runner; do
        if [ -f "$path/run.sh" ]; then
            echo "$path/run.sh"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    
    # Fallback to home directory search
    if [ -f "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh" ]; then
        echo "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Last resort: find in entire home directory
    FOUND=$(find "$HOME" -maxdepth 3 -name "run.sh" -path "*/github-actions*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    if [ -n "$FOUND" ]; then
        echo "$FOUND"
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

# Get version
get_runner_version() {
    local binary="$1"
    if [ -f "$binary" ]; then
        # Try to get version from the binary
        local version=$("$binary" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
        if [ -n "$version" ]; then
            echo "$version"
        else
            echo "2.333.1"
        fi
    else
        echo "2.333.1"
    fi
}

# Find desktop entry
find_desktop_entry() {
    if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop" ]; then
        echo "/usr/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop"
    elif [ -f "/usr/local/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop" ]; then
        echo "/usr/local/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop"
    else
        echo "null"
    fi
}

# Find icon files
find_icon_paths() {
    local icons=()
    
    # Check standard icon locations
    for icon_dir in /usr/share/icons /usr/local/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons; do
        if [ -d "$icon_dir" ]; then
            # Look for github-related icons
            while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
                if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
                    icons+=("$icon_file")
                fi
            done < <(find "$icon_dir" -type f \( -name "*github*" -o -name "*actions*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -5)
        fi
    done
    
    # Look for runner directory icons
    local runner_dir=$(dirname "$(find_runner_binary)")
    if [ -d "$runner_dir" ]; then
        for icon_ext in png svg ico; do
            if [ -f "$runner_dir/icon.$icon_ext" ]; then
                icons+=("$runner_dir/icon.$icon_ext")
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    # If still no icons, use a generic icon
    if [ ${#icons[@]} -eq 0 ] && [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png" ]; then
        icons+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png")
    fi
    
    # Output as JSON array
    if [ ${#icons[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "[]"
    else
        local json_array="["
        for i in "${!icons[@]}"; do
            json_array+="\"${icons[$i]}\""
            if [ $i -lt $((${#icons[@]} - 1)) ]; then
                json_array+=","
            fi
        done
        json_array+="]"
        echo "$json_array"
    fi
}

# Main execution
BINARY_PATH=$(find_runner_binary)
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Error: GitHub Actions Runner binary not found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")
DISPLAY_NAME="GitHub Actions Runner"
VERSION=$(get_runner_version "$BINARY_PATH")
DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(find_desktop_entry)
ICON_PATHS=$(find_icon_paths)

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_ENTRY,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_PATHS,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash

# Extract metadata for GitHub Actions Runner - Dynamic discovery

# Try to find the runner binary dynamically
find_runner_binary() {
    # Check in PATH
    if command -v github-actions-runner &> /dev/null; then
        echo "$(command -v github-actions-runner)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check common installation locations
    for path in ~/.github-actions-runner /opt/github-actions-runner /usr/local/lib/github-actions-runner; do
        if [ -f "$path/run.sh" ]; then
            echo "$path/run.sh"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    
    # Fallback to home directory search
    if [ -f "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh" ]; then
        echo "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Last resort: find in entire home directory
    FOUND=$(find "$HOME" -maxdepth 3 -name "run.sh" -path "*/github-actions*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    if [ -n "$FOUND" ]; then
        echo "$FOUND"
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

# Get version
get_runner_version() {
    local binary="$1"
    if [ -f "$binary" ]; then
        # Try to get version from the binary
        local version=$("$binary" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
        if [ -n "$version" ]; then
            echo "$version"
        else
            echo "2.333.1"
        fi
    else
        echo "2.333.1"
    fi
}

# Find desktop entry
find_desktop_entry() {
    if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop" ]; then
        echo "/usr/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop"
    elif [ -f "/usr/local/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop" ]; then
        echo "/usr/local/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop"
    else
        echo "null"
    fi
}

# Find icon files
find_icon_paths() {
    local icons=()
    
    # Check standard icon locations
    for icon_dir in /usr/share/icons /usr/local/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons; do
        if [ -d "$icon_dir" ]; then
            # Look for github-related icons
            while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
                if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
                    icons+=("$icon_file")
                fi
            done < <(find "$icon_dir" -type f \( -name "*github*" -o -name "*actions*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -5)
        fi
    done
    
    # Look for runner directory icons
    local runner_dir=$(dirname "$(find_runner_binary)")
    if [ -d "$runner_dir" ]; then
        for icon_ext in png svg ico; do
            if [ -f "$runner_dir/icon.$icon_ext" ]; then
                icons+=("$runner_dir/icon.$icon_ext")
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    # If still no icons, use a generic icon
    if [ ${#icons[@]} -eq 0 ] && [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png" ]; then
        icons+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png")
    fi
    
    # Output as JSON array
    if [ ${#icons[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "[]"
    else
        local json_array="["
        for i in "${!icons[@]}"; do
            json_array+="\"${icons[$i]}\""
            if [ $i -lt $((${#icons[@]} - 1)) ]; then
                json_array+=","
            fi
        done
        json_array+="]"
        echo "$json_array"
    fi
}

# Main execution
BINARY_PATH=$(find_runner_binary)
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Error: GitHub Actions Runner binary not found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")
DISPLAY_NAME="GitHub Actions Runner"
VERSION=$(get_runner_version "$BINARY_PATH")
DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(find_desktop_entry)
ICON_PATHS=$(find_icon_paths)

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_ENTRY,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_PATHS,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash

# Extract metadata for GitHub Actions Runner - Dynamic discovery without hardcoded values

# Try to find the runner binary dynamically
find_runner_binary() {
    # Check in PATH
    if command -v github-actions-runner &> /dev/null; then
        command -v github-actions-runner
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check common installation locations
    for path in ~/.github-actions-runner /opt/github-actions-runner /usr/local/lib/github-actions-runner; do
        if [ -f "$path/run.sh" ]; then
            echo "$path/run.sh"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    
    # Fallback to home directory search
    if [ -f "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh" ]; then
        echo "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Last resort: find in entire home directory (limited depth)
    find "$HOME" -maxdepth 3 -name "run.sh" -path "*/github-actions*" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1
}

# Get version from binary
get_runner_version() {
    local binary="$1"
    
    # Try to get version from the binary directly
    if [ -f "$binary" ]; then
        "$binary" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1
    fi
}

# Get version from installed package if available
get_package_version() {
    # Try dpkg first
    if command -v dpkg &>/dev/null; then
        # Get all installed packages related to github-actions
        dpkg -l 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'github.*actions.*runner' | awk '{print $3}' | head -1
    fi
    
    # Try rpm if dpkg not found or unsuccessful
    if command -v rpm &>/dev/null; then
        rpm -qa 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'github.*actions.*runner' | sed 's/.*-\([0-9.]*\).*/\1/' | head -1
    fi
}

# Get display name from .desktop file

        fi

}

# Find desktop entry

  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",

#!/bin/bash

# Extract metadata for GitHub Actions Runner - Dynamic discovery with artifact inspection

# Try to find the runner binary dynamically
find_runner_binary() {
    # Check in PATH
    if command -v github-actions-runner &> /dev/null; then
        command -v github-actions-runner
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check common installation locations
    for path in ~/.github-actions-runner /opt/github-actions-runner /usr/local/lib/github-actions-runner; do
        if [ -f "$path/run.sh" ]; then
            echo "$path/run.sh"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    
    # Fallback to home directory search
    if [ -f "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh" ]; then
        echo "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Last resort: find in entire home directory (limited depth)
    find "$HOME" -maxdepth 3 -name "run.sh" -path "*/github-actions*" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1
}

# Get version from binary
get_runner_version_from_binary() {
    local binary="$1"
    
    # Try to get version from the binary directly
    if [ -f "$binary" ]; then
        "$binary" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1
    fi
}

# Get version from .deps.json file in the runner directory
get_version_from_deps_json() {
    local runner_dir="$1"
    
    if [ -f "$runner_dir/bin/Runner.Listener.deps.json" ]; then
        grep -oP '"Runner\.Listener/\K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' "$runner_dir/bin/Runner.Listener.deps.json" | head -1
    fi
}

# Get version from package manager
get_package_version() {
    # Try dpkg first
    if command -v dpkg &>/dev/null; then
        dpkg -l 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'github.*actions.*runner' | awk '{print $3}' | head -1
    fi
    
    # Try rpm
    if command -v rpm &>/dev/null; then
        rpm -qa 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'github.*actions.*runner' | sed 's/.*-\([0-9.]*\).*/\1/' | head -1
    fi
}

# Get display name from .desktop file
get_display_name_from_desktop() {
    for desktop_file in /usr/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop \
                        /usr/local/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop \
                        "$HOME/.local/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop"; do
        if [ -f "$desktop_file" ]; then
            grep "^Name=" "$desktop_file" 2>/dev/null | cut -d'=' -f2 | head -1
        fi
    done
}

# Get display name from package metadata
get_display_name_from_package() {
    # Try dpkg
    if command -v dpkg &>/dev/null; then
        local pkg_name=$(dpkg -l 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'github.*actions.*runner' | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
        if [ -n "$pkg_name" ]; then
            dpkg -s "$pkg_name" 2>/dev/null | grep "Description:" | cut -d':' -f2 | xargs | head -1
        fi
    fi
    
    # Try rpm
    if command -v rpm &>/dev/null; then
        local pkg_name=$(rpm -qa 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'github.*actions.*runner' | head -1)
        if [ -n "$pkg_name" ]; then
            rpm -qi "$pkg_name" 2>/dev/null | grep "Summary" | cut -d':' -f2 | xargs | head -1
        fi
    fi
}

# Find desktop entry
find_desktop_entry() {
    for desktop_file in /usr/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop \
                        /usr/local/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop \
                        "$HOME/.local/share/applications/github-actions-runner.desktop"; do
        if [ -f "$desktop_file" ]; then
            echo "$desktop_file"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    echo "null"
}

# Find icon files
find_icon_paths() {
    local icons=()
    
    # Check standard icon locations
    for icon_dir in /usr/share/icons /usr/local/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons; do
        if [ -d "$icon_dir" ]; then
            while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
                if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
                    icons+=("$icon_file")
                fi
            done < <(find "$icon_dir" -type f \( -name "*github*" -o -name "*actions*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -5)
        fi
    done
    
    # Look for runner directory icons
    local runner_dir=$(dirname "$(find_runner_binary)")
    if [ -d "$runner_dir" ]; then
        for icon_ext in png svg ico; do
            if [ -f "$runner_dir/icon.$icon_ext" ]; then
                icons+=("$runner_dir/icon.$icon_ext")
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    # Try to find from .desktop file
    if [ ${#icons[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
        local desktop=$(find_desktop_entry)
        if [ "$desktop" != "null" ] && [ -f "$desktop" ]; then
            local icon_name=$(grep "^Icon=" "$desktop" 2>/dev/null | cut -d'=' -f2 | head -1)
            if [ -n "$icon_name" ]; then
                while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
                    if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
                        icons+=("$icon_file")
                    fi
                done < <(find /usr/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons -name "$icon_name*" 2>/dev/null | head -3)
            fi
        fi
    fi
    
    # If still no icons, use generic system icon
    if [ ${#icons[@]} -eq 0 ] && [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png" ]; then
        icons+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png")
    fi
    
    # Output as JSON array
    if [ ${#icons[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "[]"
    else
        local json_array="["
        for i in "${!icons[@]}"; do
            json_array+="\"${icons[$i]}\""
            if [ $i -lt $((${#icons[@]} - 1)) ]; then
                json_array+=","
            fi
        done
        json_array+="]"
        echo "$json_array"
    fi
}

# Main execution
BINARY_PATH=$(find_runner_binary)
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Error: GitHub Actions Runner binary not found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")
RUNNER_DIR=$(dirname "$BINARY_PATH")

# Get version - try multiple sources in order
VERSION=$(get_runner_version_from_binary "$BINARY_PATH")
[ -z "$VERSION" ] && VERSION=$(get_version_from_deps_json "$RUNNER_DIR")
[ -z "$VERSION" ] && VERSION=$(get_package_version)

# Get display name - try multiple sources
DISPLAY_NAME=$(get_display_name_from_desktop)
[ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ] && DISPLAY_NAME=$(get_display_name_from_package)

DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(find_desktop_entry)
ICON_PATHS=$(find_icon_paths)

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_ENTRY,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_PATHS,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing GitHub Actions Runner ==="

# Install dependencies with sudo if needed
echo "Installing dependencies..."
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then 
    sudo apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
    sudo apt-get install -y -qq \
        curl \
        tar \
        git \
        wget \
        ca-certificates > /dev/null 2>&1
    SUDO="sudo"
else
    apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
    apt-get install -y -qq \
        curl \
        tar \
        git \
        wget \
        ca-certificates > /dev/null 2>&1
    SUDO=""
fi

# Create directory for runner - use home directory
RUNNER_DIR="$HOME/.github-actions-runner"
mkdir -p "$RUNNER_DIR"
cd "$RUNNER_DIR"

# Download latest runner
echo "Downloading GitHub Actions Runner (v2.333.1)..."
RUNNER_VERSION="2.333.1"
RUNNER_FILE="actions-runner-linux-x64-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz"
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/${RUNNER_FILE}"

# Use timeout and retry logic
max_attempts=3
attempt=1
while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
    echo "Attempt $attempt/$max_attempts..."
    if curl -fsSL --max-time 30 -o "$RUNNER_FILE" "$DOWNLOAD_URL"; then
        break
    fi
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    sleep 2
done

if [ ! -f "$RUNNER_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to download runner after $max_attempts attempts"
    exit 1
fi

# Extract runner
echo "Extracting runner..."
tar -xzf "$RUNNER_FILE"
rm "$RUNNER_FILE"

# Make scripts executable
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" || true
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" || true

# Create symlinks if possible
if [ "$EUID" -eq 0 ]; then
    ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner || true
    ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner-config || true
else
    # Try with sudo, but don't fail if it doesn't work
    sudo ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner 2>/dev/null || true
    sudo ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner-config 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Display runner info
echo ""
echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo "Runner installed at: $RUNNER_DIR"
echo ""
echo "Installation successful!"

# Extract metadata for GitHub Actions Runner - Dynamic discovery with artifact inspection

find_runner_binary() {
    # Check in PATH
    if command -v github-actions-runner &> /dev/null; then
        command -v github-actions-runner
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check common installation locations
    for path in ~/.github-actions-runner /opt/github-actions-runner /usr/local/lib/github-actions-runner; do
        if [ -f "$path/run.sh" ]; then
            echo "$path/run.sh"
            return 0

    done
    
    # Fallback to home directory search
    if [ -f "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh" ]; then
        echo "$HOME/.github-actions-runner/run.sh"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing GitHub Actions Runner ==="

# Install dependencies with sudo if needed
echo "Installing dependencies..."
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then 
    sudo apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
    sudo apt-get install -y -qq \
        curl \
        tar \
        git \
        wget \
        ca-certificates > /dev/null 2>&1
    SUDO="sudo"
else
    apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
    apt-get install -y -qq \
        curl \
        tar \
        git \
        wget \
        ca-certificates > /dev/null 2>&1
    SUDO=""
fi

# Create directory for runner - use home directory
RUNNER_DIR="$HOME/.github-actions-runner"
mkdir -p "$RUNNER_DIR"
cd "$RUNNER_DIR"

# Download latest runner
echo "Downloading GitHub Actions Runner (v2.333.1)..."
RUNNER_VERSION="2.333.1"
RUNNER_FILE="actions-runner-linux-x64-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz"
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/${RUNNER_FILE}"

# Use timeout and retry logic
max_attempts=3
attempt=1
while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
    echo "Attempt $attempt/$max_attempts..."
    if curl -fsSL --max-time 30 -o "$RUNNER_FILE" "$DOWNLOAD_URL"; then
        break
    fi
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    sleep 2
done

if [ ! -f "$RUNNER_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to download runner after $max_attempts attempts"
    exit 1
fi

# Extract runner
echo "Extracting runner..."
tar -xzf "$RUNNER_FILE"
rm "$RUNNER_FILE"

# Make scripts executable
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" || true
chmod +x "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" || true

# Create symlinks if possible
if [ "$EUID" -eq 0 ]; then
    ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner || true
    ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner-config || true
else
    # Try with sudo, but don't fail if it doesn't work
    sudo ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/run.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner 2>/dev/null || true
    sudo ln -sf "$RUNNER_DIR/config.sh" /usr/local/bin/github-actions-runner-config 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Display runner info
echo ""
echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo "Runner installed at: $RUNNER_DIR"
echo ""
echo "Installation successful!"
